reasoning: The 'navn' field contains the official registered name of the company
The 'organisasjonsnummer' is the Norwegian organization number, serving as the unique registration identifier
Using the human-readable description (e.g., 'Stiftelse', 'Aksjeselskap') rather than the code for better downstream normalization
Status derived from boolean flags: konkurs (bankruptcy), underAvvikling (under liquidation), underTvangsavviklingEllerTvangsopplosning (forced dissolution)
This is Norwegian registry data (Brønnøysund), all companies are registered in Norway
The landkode in forretningsadresse contains the ISO country code for the business address
The landkode in postadresse contains the ISO country code for the postal address
The adresse field is an array of street address lines that should be joined
The poststed field contains the city/municipality for the business address
The adresse field in postadresse is an array of postal street address lines
The poststed field in postadresse contains the postal city/municipality
The antallAnsatte field contains the number of employees
The belop field within kapital contains the capital amount
The valuta field contains the currency code (e.g., NOK)
The fulltInnbetalt boolean indicates whether capital is fully paid up
The registreringsdatoEnhetsregisteret is the date of registration in the Entity Register
Try aktivitet (business activities) first as it describes what the company does, fall back to vedtektsfestetFormaal (statutory purpose)

For foreign entities (UTLA), this field contains the registration number from their home country, which serves as a prior/alternative registration number. Example: '5562564129' for the Swedish Siemens entity
